entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species Sorghum bicolor [L.] Moench being studied for genetic diversity.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Physical and growth-related characteristics of the sorghum genotypes.

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL QUALITY TRAITS
entities.description: Traits related to the nutritional value of the sorghum grain.

entities.name: GRAIN IRON CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of iron (Fe) in the sorghum grain.

entities.name: GRAIN ZINC CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of zinc (Zn) in the sorghum grain.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area.

entities.name: AGRO-MORPHOLOGICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Morphological characteristics of the sorghum plant used for evaluation.

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY ANALYSIS
entities.description: The process of determining the genetic variation among germplasm collections.

entities.name: FIELD EVALUATION
entities.description: The process of testing sorghum genotypes in a field setting.

entities.name: AUGMENTED DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used for the field evaluation of germplasm.

entities.name: CLUSTER ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to group sorghum genotypes based on phenotypic traits.

entities.name: HERITABILITY COMPUTATION
entities.description: The calculation of the proportion of phenotypic variance due to genetic factors.

entities.name: GENETIC ADVANCE COMPUTATION
entities.description: The calculation of the expected genetic gain from selection.

entities.name: CLIMATE-RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of the crop to withstand adverse climate conditions.

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: The broader region where sustainable food systems are essential.

entities.name: UGANDA
entities.description: The specific country where the field evaluation was conducted.

entities.name: EAST AFRICA
entities.description: The region from which the sorghum germplasm collections were sourced.

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The variation of genetic characteristics within the sorghum population.

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE FOOD SYSTEMS
entities.description: Food systems that are environmentally sound and socially just.

entities.name: STRATEGIC CONSERVATION
entities.description: The planned preservation of genetic resources for future use.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The science of changing the traits of plants to produce desired characteristics.
